pitch.title: The Echo Chamber
pitch.logline: A disgraced sound engineer hired to restore audio from a notorious 1970s cult mass suicide discovers the recordings are not just capturing the past, but actively predicting the grisly deaths of everyone who listens to them.
pitch.core_theme: The inescapable nature of guilt and the danger of obsessing over the past.
pitch.synopsis:
Act I: Arthur, a once-great audio engineer now struggling with tinnitus and guilt over a past accident, takes a lucrative job to clean up the Eden's Gate tapes for a documentary. As he isolates the tracks, he hears whispers calling his name.

Act II: The documentary director dies in the exact manner described in a newly uncovered audio track. Arthur realizes the tape is a supernatural blueprint. He tries to destroy the tapes, but the audio has already leaked to the production crew, who begin dying one by one. He must find the surviving cult leader to break the curse.

Act III: Arthur confronts the elderly cult leader in a remote cabin. He learns the only way to stop the broadcast is to deafen himself, severing his connection to the echo. In a tense, silent finale, he destroys his own hearing to survive, finally finding peace in the silence.

stage2_outline.title: The Echo Chamber
stage2_outline.logline: A disgraced sound engineer hired to restore audio from a notorious 1970s cult mass suicide discovers the recordings are not just capturing the past, but actively predicting the grisly deaths of everyone who listens to them.

beats.description: Arthur lives in a dingy apartment surrounded by analog audio gear. He suffers from severe tinnitus and debilitating guilt over a past studio accident that cost a colleague their life.

Desperate for money, Arthur accepts a lucrative job from a documentary director to restore the degraded audio tapes of the Eden's Gate cult mass suicide. Upon his first pass of the audio, he isolates a track and hears a faint whisper calling his own name.
Arthur obsessively cleans the audio tracks, uncovering a disturbing sermon where the cult leader describes a specific, gruesome death involving shattered glass.

The documentary director is killed in a freak accident involving a shattered glass window, matching the exact details of the restored audio track. Arthur realizes the tape is a supernatural blueprint for murder.
Arthur frantically analyzes the rest of the tapes to see who is next. He discovers the audio describes the deaths of the entire documentary crew.
He attempts to warn the production crew, but they dismiss him as a paranoid, guilt-ridden alcoholic suffering from auditory hallucinations.
Arthur breaks into the production office to steal and incinerate the master tapes, hoping to break the curse.

He destroys the physical tapes, but discovers the production assistant already uploaded the raw audio files to a shared cloud drive. The assistant dies horribly while listening to the files, and Arthur hears his own agonizing death predicted on the next track.
With the curse now digitized and unstoppable by normal means, Arthur watches helplessly as another crew member falls victim to the audio's prophecy.
Arthur digs into the cult's history and identifies a single surviving member of Eden's Gate who mysteriously escaped the mass suicide decades ago.
Arthur travels to a remote, off-the-grid cabin to confront the elderly cult survivor, hoping to find a way to stop the lethal broadcast.

The survivor reveals that the cult worshipped an entity that lives inside sound waves. The entity feeds on guilt and uses hearing as its doorway. The only way to stop the curse is to completely sever the connection to the echo.
The entity tracks Arthur to the cabin, manifesting as a deafening, supernatural auditory assault that uses the screams of Arthur's dead colleague to weaponize his guilt.
The elderly survivor is killed by the sonic attack. Arthur is pinned down, his ears bleeding, as the audio prophecy of his own death begins to play out in reality.
Realizing he cannot outrun the sound, Arthur grabs a pair of steel awls from the cabin's workbench and deliberately punctures his own eardrums, deafening himself before the prophecy can complete.

The supernatural storm ceases instantly for Arthur. He survives the night and is later seen living in a quiet, isolated coastal town. He is completely deaf, but for the first time in his life, he is free of his guilt and has finally found peace in the silence.
